name: Full-Stack Application
description: Complete AGENTS.md for a modern full-stack web application.
content:
# AGENTS.md

> This file defines the rules, conventions, and expectations for AI coding agents working in this full-stack application repository.

---

## Table of Contents

- [Frontend Development Guidelines](#frontend-development-guidelines)
- [Accessibility (a11y) Requirements](#accessibility-a11y-requirements)
- [Frontend Performance Standards](#frontend-performance-standards)
- [Backend API Design Standards](#backend-api-design-standards)
- [Error Handling & Logging](#error-handling--logging)
- [Database Interaction Guidelines](#database-interaction-guidelines)
- [Testing Strategy](#testing-strategy)
- [Coverage Requirements](#coverage-requirements)
- [Test Writing Best Practices](#test-writing-best-practices)
- [Code Comment Standards](#code-comment-standards)
- [README Standards](#readme-standards)
- [API Documentation Standards](#api-documentation-standards)

---

## Frontend Development Guidelines

This section governs all frontend code produced or modified by AI agents.

### Technology Stack

- **Framework**: React 18+ with TypeScript. Do not introduce Vue, Angular, or Svelte without explicit approval.
- **Styling**: Tailwind CSS. Keep utility classes in JSX; use `@apply` in CSS only for complex reusable patterns.
- **State Management**: Zustand for global state. React Query for server state. Local `useState` for UI-only state.
- **Build Tool**: Vite. Do not modify `vite.config.ts` without a dedicated PR.

### Component Design

1. Single Responsibility — one component, one concern.
2. Define TypeScript interfaces for all props.
3. Prefer controlled form components.
4. Use composition patterns (children, render props) over inheritance.
5. Components over 300 lines must be split.

### File Naming

- Components: `PascalCase.tsx`
- Hooks: `use-kebab-case.ts`
- Utils: `camelCase.ts`
- Tests: `ComponentName.test.tsx` co-located with component

---

## Accessibility (a11y) Requirements

All UI must meet WCAG 2.1 AA. Use semantic HTML, ARIA labels, keyboard navigation, sufficient colour contrast (4.5:1), and visible focus indicators. Run `axe-core` in CI.

---

## Frontend Performance Standards

Target: LCP < 2.5s, INP < 200ms, CLS < 0.1. Use dynamic imports for routes, `loading="lazy"` for images, and avoid synchronous heavy operations on the main thread.

---

## Backend API Design Standards

RESTful API with `/api/v1/` prefix. Resource names are plural nouns. Use correct HTTP methods and status codes. All errors follow the standard envelope: `{ error: { code, message, details, requestId } }`.

---

## Error Handling & Logging

Never swallow errors silently. Use structured JSON logging with `requestId` correlation. Never expose stack traces in production. Implement retries with exponential backoff for external service calls.

---

## Database Interaction Guidelines

Always use parameterised queries. Wrap multi-step operations in transactions. All schema changes are versioned migration files. Add indexes for foreign keys and WHERE/ORDER BY columns.

---

## Testing Strategy

Follow the test pyramid: ~70% unit, ~20% integration, ~10% E2E. Every new function requires at least one test in the same PR. Use Jest + React Testing Library for frontend, Supertest for API integration tests, Playwright for E2E.

---

## Coverage Requirements

Minimum: 80% lines, 75% branches, 80% functions. Coverage drops of more than 2% block merge. Measure `src/` only.

---

## Test Writing Best Practices

Use the AAA pattern (Arrange-Act-Assert). Name tests as sentences. Mock at the boundary. Never use `sleep()` — use explicit waits. Fix flaky tests immediately.

---

## Code Comment Standards

Comment the *why*, not the *what*. All public functions require JSDoc with `@param`, `@returns`, and `@example`. Every `TODO` must reference an issue number.

---

## README Standards

README must include: description, badges, getting started, usage examples, configuration table, contributing guide, and license. Update in the same PR as the code change.

---

## API Documentation Standards

All HTTP APIs documented with OpenAPI 3.1 (`openapi.yaml`). Validate with `spectral lint` in CI. Every endpoint must include summary, all parameters, all response codes, and at least one example.
